channel.title: Serenity News Briefing
channel.description: Curated news briefings — Iran conflict, NYC politics, AI/ML, fintech

item.title: Briefing — March 4, 2026
item.description:
Iran / US-Iran Conflict

Israel launches new strikes on Iran; first US soldiers killed. Four US Army reserve soldiers killed in a drone strike in Kuwait — first American military casualties since joint operation began. (BBC)

Funerals held after Iran school strike. Iranian authorities report students and staff killed in a US-Israeli strike in Minab. Civilian casualties mounting. (BBC)

Gulf states weigh response to Iranian strikes. Retaliatory Iranian strikes on Gulf states have damaged their stability image. Regional powers deliberating response. (BBC)

NYC — Mayor Mamdani

Mamdani to reinstitute homeless encampment sweeps. Dept. of Homeless Services replaces NYPD as lead on sweeps — policy shift from Adams era. (NYT)

Mamdani threatens 9.5% property tax hike if wealth tax fails. Framing property tax increase as "last resort" to close budget gap; pressuring Albany on wealth tax. (NYT)

AI / ML

Claude Code rolls out voice mode. Anthropic's Claude Code now supports voice-based interactions for developers. (TechCrunch)

Alibaba's Qwen tech lead steps down. Leadership churn at one of China's major AI initiatives after a significant dev push. (TechCrunch)

AI startups selling same equity at two prices. Growing trend of divergent valuations for identical equity in AI fundraising rounds. (TechCrunch)

Fintech

Stripe wants to turn AI costs into a profit center. New tools to help companies monetize AI infrastructure spend. (TechCrunch)

PayPay IPO stalled by geopolitical tensions. SoftBank-backed fintech's planned IPO delayed amid conflict-driven market uncertainty. (TechCrunch)

Plaid valued at $8B in employee share sale. Strong signal for financial data connectivity space. (TechCrunch)
